### Bash - 2026-03-15 17:08:31 UTC [18:08:31 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -lt /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -10",
  "description": "List recent LSL files"
}
```

  "old_string": "    if (currentStepAgentId) {\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentStepAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx >= 0) {\n        // All agents before current are completed\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          const priorStatus = statusMap[priorAgent]?.status\n          if (!priorStatus || priorStatus === 'pending' || priorStatus === 'running') {\n            agentSet.add(priorAgent)\n            statusMap[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as StepInfo\n          }\n        }\n        // Current agent is running (unless already completed from a previous wave)\n        const currentStatus = statusMap[currentStepAgentId]?.status\n        if (!currentStatus || currentStatus === 'pending') {\n          agentSet.add(currentStepAgentId)\n          statusMap[currentStepAgentId] = { name: currentStepAgentId, status: 'running' } as StepInfo\n        }\n      }\n    }",
  "new_string": "    if (currentStepAgentId) {\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentStepAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx >= 0) {\n        // All agents before current are completed (in this wave)\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          agentSet.add(priorAgent)\n          statusMap[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as StepInfo\n        }\n        // Current agent is running — override even if 'completed' from a prior wave\n        agentSet.add(currentStepAgentId)\n        statusMap[currentStepAgentId] = { name: currentStepAgentId, status: 'running' } as StepInfo\n        // Agents after current haven't run in this wave yet — reset to pending\n        // even if they were 'completed' from a prior wave\n        for (let i = currentIdx + 1; i < WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.length; i++) {\n          const futureAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          agentSet.add(futureAgent)\n          statusMap[futureAgent] = { name: futureAgent, status: 'pending' } as StepInfo\n        }\n      }\n    }"
}

### Bash - 2026-03-15 17:08:31 UTC [18:08:31 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -lt /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -10",
  "description": "List recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```

-rw-r--

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

}

  "old_string": "    if (currentStepAgentId) {\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentStepAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx >= 0) {\n        // All agents before current are completed\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          const priorStatus = statusMap[priorAgent]?.status\n          if (!priorStatus || priorStatus === 'pending' || priorStatus === 'running') {\n            agentSet.add(priorAgent)\n            statusMap[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as StepInfo\n          }\n        }\n        // Current agent is running (unless already completed from a previous wave)\n        const currentStatus = statusMap[currentStepAgentId]?.status\n        if (!currentStatus || currentStatus === 'pending') {\n          agentSet.add(currentStepAgentId)\n          statusMap[currentStepAgentId] = { name: currentStepAgentId, status: 'running' } as StepInfo\n        }\n      }\n    }",
  "new_string": "    if (currentStepAgentId) {\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentStepAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx >= 0) {\n        // All agents before current are completed (in this wave)\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          agentSet.add(priorAgent)\n          statusMap[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as StepInfo\n        }\n        // Current agent is running — override even if 'completed' from a prior wave\n        agentSet.add(currentStepAgentId)\n        statusMap[currentStepAgentId] = { name: currentStepAgentId, status: 'running' } as StepInfo\n        // Agents after current haven't run in this wave yet — reset to pending\n        // even if they were 'completed' from a prior wave\n        for (let i = currentIdx + 1; i < WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.length; i++) {\n          const futureAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          agentSet.add(futureAgent)\n          statusMap[futureAgent] = { name: futureAgent, status: 'pending' } as StepInfo\n        }\n      }\n    }"
}

### Text Exchange - 2026-03-15 17:23:03 UTC [18:23:03 CEST]

**Assistant Response:** No auto-switch exists. Let me implement the fix: use a time-based window to keep completed workflows visible briefly, then remove them from Active.


**Type:** Text-only exchange (no tool calls)

---

### Edit - 2026-03-15 17:23:03 UTC [18:23:03 CEST]

**System Action:** (Initiated automatically)

**Tool:** Edit
**Input:** ```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/system-health-dashboard/src/components/ukb-workflow-modal.tsx",
  "old_string": "  // Filter processes for the Active tab\n  // ONLY show workflows that are actually running - cancelled/completed/failed go to History\n  // FIX: Previously included ALL isInlineMCP workflows regardless of status, causing \"zombie\" workflows\n  const activeProcesses = useMemo(() => {\n    return processes.filter(p => {\n      // Only consider truly active states\n      const isActiveStatus = p.status === 'running' || p.status === 'starting' || p.status === 'cancelling'\n      // For non-inline processes, also check isAlive flag\n      const isAlive = p.isAlive && p.status !== 'completed' && p.status !== 'failed' && p.status !== 'cancelled'\n      // ALSO keep recently completed/failed processes so the trace doesn't go blank\n      // when a workflow finishes while the user is watching\n      const isTerminalWithSteps = (p.status === 'completed' || p.status === 'failed') &&\n        p.steps && p.steps.length > 0\n      return isActiveStatus || isAlive || isTerminalWithSteps\n    })\n    // Include processesSignature to ensure recalculation when any process data changes\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [processes, processesSignature])",
  "new_string": "  // Track when workflows first enter terminal state so we can show them briefly before moving to History\n  const terminalTimestampsRef = useRef<Map<string, number>>(new Map())\n\n  // Filter processes for the Active tab\n  // ONLY show workflows that are actually running - cancelled/completed/failed go to History\n  // Terminal workflows stay in Active for 10s so the trace doesn't go blank instantly\n  const activeProcesses = useMemo(() => {\n    const now = Date.now()\n    return processes.filter(p => {\n      // Only consider truly active states\n      const isActiveStatus = p.status === 'running' || p.status === 'starting' || p.status === 'cancelling'\n      // For non-inline processes, also check isAlive flag\n      const isAlive = p.isAlive && p.status !== 'completed' && p.status !== 'failed' && p.status !== 'cancelled'\n      // Keep recently completed/failed workflows for 10s so the trace doesn't go blank\n      const isTerminal = (p.status === 'completed' || p.status === 'failed' || p.status === 'cancelled')\n      let isRecentlyTerminal = false\n      if (isTerminal && p.steps && p.steps.length > 0) {\n        const processKey = (p as any).workflowId || p.workflowName || 'default'\n        if (!terminalTimestampsRef.current.has(processKey)) {\n          terminalTimestampsRef.current.set(processKey, now)\n        }\n        const terminalSince = terminalTimestampsRef.current.get(processKey)!\n        isRecentlyTerminal = (now - terminalSince) < 10_000\n      }\n      if (!isTerminal) {\n        // Clean up timestamp if process is no longer terminal (restarted)\n        const processKey = (p as any).workflowId || p.workflowName || 'default'\n        terminalTimestampsRef.current.delete(processKey)\n      }\n      return isActiveStatus || isAlive || isRecentlyTerminal\n    })\n    // Include processesSignature to ensure recalculation when any process data changes\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [processes, processesSignature])"
}
```

  "old_string": "  // Auto-select orchestrator/coordinator when modal opens with active workflows\n  // This ensures the sidebar is visible immediately showing workflow overview\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (open && activeTab === 'active' && activeProcesses.length > 0 && selectedNode === null) {\n      Logger.info(LogCategories.UI, 'Auto-selecting orchestrator node on modal open')\n      dispatch(setSelectedNode('orchestrator'))\n    }\n  }, [open, activeTab, activeProcesses.length, selectedNode, dispatch])",
  "new_string": "  // Auto-select orchestrator/coordinator when modal opens with active workflows\n  // This ensures the sidebar is visible immediately showing workflow overview\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (open && activeTab === 'active' && activeProcesses.length > 0 && selectedNode === null) {\n      Logger.info(LogCategories.UI, 'Auto-selecting orchestrator node on modal open')\n      dispatch(setSelectedNode('orchestrator'))\n    }\n  }, [open, activeTab, activeProcesses.length, selectedNode, dispatch])\n\n  // Auto-switch to History tab when Active tab becomes empty (workflow completed)\n  const prevActiveCountRef = useRef(activeProcesses.length)\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (open && activeTab === 'active' && prevActiveCountRef.current > 0 && activeProcesses.length === 0) {\n      Logger.info(LogCategories.UI, 'Active tab empty after workflow completed, switching to History')\n      loadHistoricalWorkflows()\n      dispatch(setActiveTab('history'))\n    }\n    prevActiveCountRef.current = activeProcesses.length\n  }, [open, activeTab, activeProcesses.length, dispatch])"
